api: AWS DynamoDB JSON HTTP API (DynamoDB_20120810 protocol, X-Amz-Target header dispatch) -- not a REST API
scope: Migration of legacy's single implemented operation (DynamoDB_20120810.Scan, paginated via LastEvaluatedKey/ExclusiveStartKey), matching internal/connectors/dynamodb exactly. DynamoDB's full documented API surface (Query, GetItem, BatchGetItem, PutItem/UpdateItem/DeleteItem writes, table/index administration, DynamoDB Streams CDC) is out of scope until Pass B; legacy itself is also read-only (Write always returns ErrUnsupportedOperation) and never implemented Query/GetItem/BatchGetItem. Every covered/excluded entry below is declared method GET (not the literal wire HTTP verb, which is always POST with an X-Amz-Target header for every DynamoDB JSON-protocol operation) to correctly signal READ semantics to connectorgen validate's capabilities.write/read cross-check (design SS E.1 rule 4) -- matching bing-ads's and monday's identical convention for their POST-only wire protocols. Genuine mutation/administrative operations keep their real semantics annotated in the reason text since they are excluded, not covered.

excluded.reason: Pass B capability expansion; not read by legacy

Pass B capability expansion; not read by legacy (legacy's Catalog is a static single-stream constant, no live DescribeTable call)
Pass B capability expansion; dynamodb is read-only in this migration (legacy Write returns ErrUnsupportedOperation)
